cards.definition: A street dance style focused on rhythm, grooves, freestyle, and personal expression. Includes elements like popping, locking, breaking, and house influences.
Combines ballet, modern, and jazz techniques to emphasize emotion, storytelling, fluid movement, and floor work.
A highly technical and disciplined style originating in Europe, characterized by grace, turnout, precise footwork, and formal positions.
An energetic style featuring strong technique, sharp movements, turns, kicks, and expressive performance quality.
Dancers wear shoes with metal plates to create rhythmic sounds, turning movement into percussion.
A rebellion against classical ballet traditions that emphasizes natural movement, contraction, release, and emotional expression.
A fusion of ballet and jazz that focuses on interpreting song lyrics and conveying emotion through movement.
An original element of hip hop culture featuring power moves, freezes, footwork, and athletic acrobatics.
A funk style where dancers rapidly contract and relax muscles to create sharp "pops" and robotic effects.
A playful funk dance involving exaggerated movements, freezes, points, and expressive performance.
Originating in club culture, house emphasizes fast footwork, grooves, improvisation, and musicality to electronic music.
An intense, high-energy street dance focused on raw emotion, powerful movements, and freestyle battles.
A disco-era dance characterized by rapid arm movements, poses, musical interpretation, and dramatic performance.
Developed within ballroom culture, featuring model-like poses, angular movements, floor work, and storytelling.
A collection of partner dances such as the waltz, foxtrot, and quickstep, emphasizing elegance and connection.
Includes styles like salsa, cha-cha, rumba, and samba, known for rhythmic footwork, hip action, and partner work.
A vibrant social dance from Latin America featuring fast footwork, spins, and strong musical connection.
An intimate partner dance originating in Argentina, characterized by dramatic expression and precise movements.
A Spanish dance form combining intricate footwork, hand movements, singing, and guitar accompaniment to express deep emotion.
Known for rapid, precise footwork while maintaining a relatively still upper body, popularized worldwide through stage productions.
A modern style focused on synchronization, performance quality, clean execution, and choreography inspired by multiple genres.
Dance designed to tell stories on stage, blending acting, singing, jazz, tap, ballet, and character work.
Combines dance, jumps, stunts, and pom routines with an emphasis on energy, precision, and teamwork.
The style seen in music videos, concerts, and television, drawing heavily from hip hop, jazz, and contemporary influences.
Traditional dances that represent the history, values, and customs of specific communities and countries, such as Māori haka, Scottish Highland dancing, or Indian Bharatanatyam.
